name: Gravel Northwest Greenway
name_original: Vía Verde del Noroeste en gravel
description:
This is one of the quintessential gravel courses in the Murcia region.

The greenway runs along the old route of the Murcia-Caravaca de la Cruz railway line, which was active until the mid-1970s.

Along its route you will cross nine beautiful viaducts and six tunnels with artificial lighting; in addition to passing by the old stations, many of them now used as hostels.

You can do the Route in an ascending direction from Murcia Capital to Caravaca de la Cruz, totaling almost 89 kilometers and just over 1000 meters of positive difference in altitude, or do it in the opposite direction and enjoy the descent. The real challenge is to do it there and back, approaching 180 kilometers and 1,500 meters of elevation gain.

I advise you to come in spring and enjoy the mild temperatures and the almond trees in bloom that you will find along much of the route.
To facilitate possible connections with public transport, the Route begins at the FFCC station in Murcia capital and ends at the bus station in Caravaca de la Cruz.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Rio Segura offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Segura River, which carves through canyons and forms unique geological features. The region features mountainous terrain, including peaks like El Relojero, alongside serene riverside paths and agricultural areas. Erosive processes have created formations such as the Salto del Usero waterfalls and Monte Arabí, a World Heritage Site known for its distinctive rock formations. This varied topography provides a range of gravel biking experiences across the Alicante-Murcia region. Best gravel bike trails around Rio Segura The most popular gravel bike trail is Gravel Northwest Greenway, a 54.8 miles (88.2 km) trail that takes 7 hours 54 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the region's varied terrain, including sections of the Vía Verde del Noroeste. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is Puente Rojo – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Ceutí, a moderate 38.2 miles (61.5 km) path. This route follows the Segura River, offering views of its banks and surrounding agricultural areas. Local gravel bikers also love the La Contraparada of the Segura River – Contraparada Park Bridge loop from Murcia del Carmen, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail leading through riverside parks and along the Segura River, often completed in about 3 hours 13 minutes. Gravel biking around Rio Segura is defined by river valleys, canyons, and mountainous sections. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate riverside loops to more challenging climbs. The routes in Rio Segura are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 20 reviews. More than 600 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Rio Segura's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many gravel bike trails are available in the Rio Segura region?
A: The Rio Segura region offers a wide variety of gravel biking experiences, with over 70 routes available on komoot. These range from moderate riverside loops to more challenging mountain climbs, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about the Rio Segura region?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from nearly 20 reviews. Gravel bikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from serene riverside paths to challenging mountainous sections, and the unique geological features found along the routes.

Q: Are there gravel bike routes suitable for beginners in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, there are several routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38131719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River – Contraparada Park Bridge loop from Murcia del Carmen&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 27.3-mile (44.0 km) trail that follows riverside parks and the Segura River, offering a pleasant experience.

Q: Can I find challenging gravel bike trails in Rio Segura?
A: Absolutely. The region features numerous difficult routes that include significant elevation gains and varied terrain. A prime example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e833524574/gravel-northwest-greenway" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gravel Northwest Greenway&lt;/a&gt;, a demanding 54.8-mile (88.2 km) trail that explores the region's diverse landscapes, including sections of the Vía Verde del Noroeste.

Q: Are there any long-distance gravel biking options in Rio Segura?
A: For those seeking longer rides, the Rio Segura region has several options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Segura River Bridge – Segura River Riverside Path loop from Murcia del Carmen&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 111.6 km route, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38134759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Rojo – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Beniel&lt;/a&gt; covers over 100 km, offering extensive exploration of the river and surrounding areas.

Q: Are there circular gravel bike routes available in Rio Segura?
A: Many of the gravel bike routes in Rio Segura are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31023593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Rojo – Murcia Río Riverside Walk loop from Ceutí&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23742002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garruchal Pass – El Garruchal Pass loop from Murcia del Carmen&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for your ride.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while gravel biking along the Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region is rich in natural beauty. You can expect to encounter impressive geological formations like the Almadenes canyon, mountainous terrain including El Relojero Peak, and unique erosional landscapes such as Monte Arabí in Yecla, a World Heritage Site. The routes often follow the Segura River, offering views of its banks and surrounding orchards.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by gravel bike in the Rio Segura area?
A: Yes, the region offers cultural points of interest. You can visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2746743" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of Hope&lt;/a&gt;, nestled within caves along the Segura River. Additionally, Monte Arabí is not only a geological wonder but also recognized for its ancient rock paintings, adding a historical dimension to your ride.

Q: Can I find routes with viewpoints in the Rio Segura region?
A: Yes, the region offers several stunning viewpoints. The Alto de Bayna viewing point in Blanca provides spectacular views over the Azud reservoir and the River Segura. Many routes also ascend to areas like El Relojero Peak, offering panoramic vistas of the diverse landscape.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to visit on a gravel bike ride in Rio Segura?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1345858" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto del Usero&lt;/a&gt; waterfalls are a notable natural monument in the region. While not directly on every gravel route, they are a popular spot resulting from fascinating erosive processes, creating a beautiful cascading water feature that is worth exploring nearby.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Rio Segura?
A: The Rio Segura region generally enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for gravel biking. During these seasons, temperatures are milder, and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summers can be very hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are recommended if riding then, while winters are generally mild.

Q: Are there family-friendly gravel biking options in Rio Segura?
A: While many routes offer varied terrain, some of the flatter, riverside sections are more suitable for families. Look for routes that stick closer to the Segura River, such as parts of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38131719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Contraparada of the Segura River – Contraparada Park Bridge loop from Murcia del Carmen&lt;/a&gt;, which pass through parks and along the riverbanks, offering a gentler experience.

Q: Is public transport available to access gravel bike trails in Rio Segura?
A: Access to trails via public transport can vary depending on the specific starting point. Major towns like Murcia, which serve as starting points for several routes, typically have bus and train connections. It's advisable to check local public transport schedules and bike carriage policies for your chosen route's trailhead.

Q: Where can I find parking near gravel bike trails in Rio Segura?
A: Many trailheads, especially those starting from towns or popular recreational areas, offer designated parking. For routes originating in or near cities like Murcia, you can often find public parking facilities. For more remote starting points, look for small parking areas or consider parking in the nearest village and cycling to the trailhead.
tips: A sample of the beautiful architecture that accompanied this line of the old Murcia-Caravaca Railway, now refurbished as a Vía Verde.

One of the most beautiful segments of the Greenway.
Fill up your water in Bullas or in Cehegín because you will not enter where to do it in between.
In summer it is always nice to feel the cool when crossing it.
